talk_metadata.talk_title: Mechanisms of ADC resistance informing novel combination therapy strategies
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped OS data entirely, presented only PFS

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: TOP1 mutations (e.g., E418K, R364H) that prevent drug intercalation
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: We observed TOP1 mutations emerging at progression, but the exact trigger remains unclear
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some patients showed prolonged responses before rapid progression, indicating a subset of exceptional responders

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Trop-2 missense mutation that shuttles the protein intracellularly, reducing surface binding
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: The missense mutant Trop-2 remained entirely intracellular and conferred resistance

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Combine topoisomerase-1 inhibitor payload with PARP inhibition to enhance DNA damage
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No biomarker stratification for TOP1 or Trop-2 status; mixed prior therapies
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline TOP1 mutation status or Trop-2 expression level
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA monitoring of emerging TOP1 mutations during treatment
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Non‑randomized, small cohort limits efficacy interpretation

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: SG payload (SN‑38) linked via cleavable, hydrolyzable linker
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the linker is labile, allowing extracellular release and bystander effect, which may also affect normal tissues
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad use without target expression selection dilutes signal and may increase toxicity

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that TOP1 mutations confer resistance, then concluded that the combination with PARP inhibitor is broadly effective despite not addressing the underlying TOP1 resistance mechanism

key_data_gaps: No overall survival data for the SG + talazoparib combination

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop computational models to predict emergence of TOP1 resistance mutations from cfDNA and design next‑generation ADCs with alternative payloads
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that TOP1 mutations were a harbinger of progression and that the combination with PARP inhibition did not fully overcome this resistance
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate longitudinal cfDNA sequencing with structural modeling of TOP1 to flag patients likely to develop resistance and suggest payload swaps
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: NCT04165772 (SG + talazoparib) for cfDNA monitoring
PubMed: studies on TOP1 resistance mutations in ADCs
SEC filings: pipeline status of alternative topoisomerase‑1 inhibitor ADCs

crispro_opportunity.description: Implement prospective Trop‑2 expression and TOP1 mutation screening to enrich for patients likely to benefit from SG
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker emphasized that Trop‑2 expression correlates only roughly with PFS and that TOP1 mutations drive resistance
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate a composite biomarker score combining IHC, RNA‑seq, and cfDNA data to guide patient selection
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: trials with biomarker‑driven SG cohorts
TCGA/GENIE datasets for Trop‑2 and TOP1 alteration frequencies

cited_competitors.name: Enfortumab vedotin
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as a successful ADC in bladder cancer to illustrate why studying resistance to effective ADCs matters

cited_competitors.name: Mylotarg
cited_competitors.context: Cited as a failed ADC that will not be discussed

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal sequencing of SG with PARP inhibitors to maximize efficacy while minimizing myelosuppression?
Can serial cfDNA monitoring reliably predict TOP1 mutation–driven resistance before clinical progression?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Tumor intrinsic and extrinsic mechanisms of ADC resistance in bladder cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't know yet whether pre‑treatment PPARγ or retinoic signatures fully predict nectin‑4 levels
Skipped detailed efficacy numbers for the priming strategy, focusing on mechanistic proof‑of‑concept

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low or heterogeneous nectin‑4 expression driven by luminal‑basal differentiation state
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: We see patients with low nectin‑4 who progress despite EV, and the underlying regulation remains incompletely defined
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Patients with nectin‑4 amplification show exceptionally high responses, indicating a subset of highly sensitive responders

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Altered endosomal trafficking and reduced ADC internalization despite retained nectin‑4 surface levels
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: EV‑resistant cells retain high nectin‑4 but take up the antibody much less frequently

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: EV monotherapy pivotal trial (Rosenberg et al.)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Targeting nectin‑4 with MMAE payload in metastatic urothelial carcinoma
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled patients regardless of nectin‑4 expression level, mixing low and high expressors in the same arm
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Pre‑treatment nectin‑4 H‑score stratification for randomization
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Subgroup analyses show wide confidence intervals for low‑expressor cohort, risking dilution of signal

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: EV linker‑MMAE construct with cleavable valine‑citrulline linker
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that the same linker chemistry is used in multiple ADCs, making it a potential class effect
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a single target (nectin‑4) without robust biomarker selection limits efficacy across heterogeneous tumors

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that PPARγ agonists up‑regulate nectin‑4 expression in tumor cells, then concluded that priming with rosiglitazone will not increase toxicity because normal tissues lack PPARγ, despite acknowledging that skin toxicity is a known issue with nectin‑4 ADCs

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective clinical trial data testing rosiglitazone priming in patients
No direct comparison of EV efficacy in nectin‑4‑amplified vs non‑amplified cohorts in a randomized setting

crispro_opportunity.description: Create a predictive model integrating PPARγ/retinoic acid pathway activity and nectin‑4 expression to select patients for EV therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated that rosiglitazone induces nectin‑4 in low‑expressing bladder cancer cells and improves ADC killing in vitro
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can combine transcriptomic signatures of PPARγ activation with IHC data to generate a composite score for patient enrollment
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: design a biomarker‑driven EV trial (e.g., NCTXXXXXX)
GTEx database analysis for normal tissue PPARγ/nectin‑4 co‑expression
PubMed: studies on PPARγ agonists in cancer

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage PPARγ agonists (e.g., rosiglitazone) or retinoids (ATRA, bexarotene) as priming agents to up‑regulate nectin‑4 before EV administration
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Rosiglitazone treatment increased surface nectin‑4 and enhanced EV‑mediated tumor suppression in cell culture
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate dosing schedules and predict optimal priming duration to maximize target up‑regulation while minimizing off‑target toxicity
external_validation_needed: Preclinical pharmacokinetic studies of rosiglitazone + EV
Safety data on combined use from FDA adverse event reports

crispro_opportunity.description: Re‑engineer EV‑resistant, nectin‑4‑high cells with CAR‑T constructs targeting the same antigen to bypass payload resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: CAR‑T cells using the EV binder killed EV‑resistant cells despite their resistance to the MMAE payload
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model antigen density thresholds required for CAR‑T activation versus ADC internalization, guiding patient selection for CAR‑T salvage
external_validation_needed: In‑vitro cytotoxicity data comparing ADC vs CAR‑T in resistant lines

cited_competitors.name: Enfortumab vedotin
cited_competitors.context: Primary ADC discussed; efficacy and resistance data presented

cited_competitors.name: Sacituzumab govitecan (SG)
cited_competitors.context: Referenced as a contrasting Trop‑2 ADC with different payload and resistance mechanisms

cited_competitors.name: HER2‑targeted ADCs (e.g., T‑DXd)
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned in comparison of target expression relevance

unresolved_questions: Can PPARγ or retinoic acid priming be safely translated to the clinic without exacerbating skin toxicity?
What are the dominant mechanisms driving EV resistance in patients who retain high nectin‑4 expression?
